title: Korbin Sims

{{short description|Fiji international rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2023}} {{Use Australian English|date=July 2013}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Korbin Sims |fullname = |image = Korbin Sims.jpg |imagesize = 290 |birth_date = {{birth date and age|df=yes|1992|01|02}} |birth_place = [[Gerringong, New South Wales]], Australia |height = {{convert|1.89|m|ftin|abbr=on|order=flip}} |weight = {{convert|108|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} |position = {{rlp|PR|LF|SR}} |club1 = [[Newcastle Knights]] |year1start = 2013 |year1end = 16 |appearances1 = 76 |tries1 = 7 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 28 |club2 = [[Brisbane Broncos]] |year2start = 2017 |year2end = 18 |appearances2 = 44 |tries2 = 9 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 36 |club3 = [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] |year3start = 2019 |year3end = 20 |appearances3 = 21 |tries3 = 2 |goals3 = 0 |fieldgoals3 = 0 |points3 = 8 |club4 = [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] |year4start = 2021 |year4end = 22 |appearances4 = 32 |tries4 = 1 |goals4 = 0 |fieldgoals4 = 0 |points4 = 0 |teamA = [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] |yearAstart = 2013 |yearAend = present |appearancesA = 12 |triesA = 3 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 12 |retired = yes |source = {{Cite web |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/korbin-sims/summary.html |title=Korbin Sims – Career Stats & Summary |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=2015-05-13}} |updated = 5 November 2022 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Ashton Sims]] (brother)
[[Tariq Sims]] (brother)
[[Ruan Sims]] (sister)
[[Reagan Campbell-Gillard]] (cousin) }} }} '''Korbin Sims''' (born 2 January 1992) is a former [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr|lf}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]], [[Brisbane Broncos]] and the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. ==Background== Sims was born in [[Gerringong, New South Wales]], Australia, and is of [[Fijians|Fijian]] descent.{{Cite web |url=https://www.mamamia.com.au/jacqueline-sims-rugby-league/ |title=Jacqueline Sims has raised five kids who are all elite sports stars. Now it's her turn to shine. |last=Jepsen |first=Belinda |date=2017-10-19 |website=Mamamia |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180123074338/https://www.mamamia.com.au/jacqueline-sims-rugby-league/ |archive-date=2018-01-23}} Sims is the younger brother of [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] player [[Tariq Sims]] and former player [[Ashton Sims]]. His elder sister [[Ruan Sims]] played for the [[Australia women's national rugby league team|Australian women's team]] in 2010 and 2015.{{Cite news |last=Walter |first=Brad |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/sims-sister-ruan-leading-way-as-female-participation-swells-20130517-2jrkx.html |title=Sims sister Ruan leading way as female participation swells |date=2013-05-17 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |access-date=2014-07-16 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180719173633/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/sims-sister-ruan-leading-way-as-female-participation-swells-20130517-2jrkx.html |archive-date=2018-07-19 |url-access=limited}} ==Playing career== ===Early years=== Sims played his junior football for the [[Gerringong Lions]] and [[Wests Panthers]] before being signed by the [[Brisbane Broncos]], along with his brothers, Ashton and Tariq in 2008. Sims played for the Queensland U16's and U18's teams.{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/sims-ready-to-defy-family-ties/tabid/10874/newsid/75926/default.aspx |title=Sims ready to defy family ties |last=Webeck |first=Tony |date=2014-01-19 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140122010659/http://www.nrl.com/sims-ready-to-defy-family-ties/tabid/10874/newsid/75926/default.aspx |archive-date=2014-01-22 |access-date=2015-05-13}} He played for the Broncos [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2010, playing alongside his brother Tariq, scoring 2 tries in 9 games.{{Cite web |url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/s/sims_korbin/index.php |title=Korbin SIMS [Player Stats] |website=The World of Rugby League |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120604224012/http://rleague.com/db/player/s/sims_korbin/index.php |archive-date=2012-06-04}} In 2011, Sims joined the [[Newcastle Knights]], playing in the NYC team in 2011 and 2012, scoring 14 tries in 40 games. [[File:korbinsims.jpg|thumb|right|Sims playing for the Knights in 2012]] In 2012, Sims was named at {{rlp|pr}} in the inaugural Under 20's [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] match for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]],{{Cite news |last=Keeble |first=Brett |url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/115297/blues-lose-korbin-sims-to-queensland/ |title=Blues lose Korbin Sims to Queensland |date=2012-04-16 |work=Newcastle Herald |access-date=2015-05-13 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140529124019/http://www.theherald.com.au/story/115297/blues-lose-korbin-sims-to-queensland/ |archive-date=2014-05-29 }} but withdrew due to a broken hand. Sims was named at prop in the 2012 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]] Team of the Year.{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/69388/default.aspx |title=Toyota Cup Team of the Year |date=2012-08-21 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180617043219/https://www.nrl.com/news/2012/08/21/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/ |archive-date=2018-06-17 |access-date=2015-05-13}} On 2 October 2012, Sims was named at {{rlp|sr}} in the [[Junior Kangaroos]] team to face the [[Junior Kiwis]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-side-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/70149/default.aspx |title=Junior Kangaroos side announced |date=2012-10-02 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121003072430/http://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-side-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/70149/default.aspx |archive-date=2012-10-03 |access-date=2015-05-13}} ===2013=== In Round 3 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Sims made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Newcastle club against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] off the interchange bench, playing against his brother [[Ashton Sims|Ashton]] in Newcastle's 34–6 win at [[Hunter Stadium]].{{Cite news |last=Keeble |first=Brett |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/thurstons-200th-game-overshadowed-by-bigscoring-knights-20130325-2gqgi.html |title=Thurston's 200th game overshadowed by big-scoring Knights |date=2013-03-25 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |access-date=2015-05-13 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180719203438/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/thurstons-200th-game-overshadowed-by-bigscoring-knights-20130325-2gqgi.html |archive-date=2018-07-19 |url-access=limited}} On 3 June, Sims re-signed with the Newcastle side on a two-year contract after rejecting offers from North Queensland and the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.smh.com.au//breaking-news-sport/korbin-sims-resigns-with-knights-20130603-2nlso.html |title=Korbin Sims re-signs with Knights |date=2013-06-03 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |access-date=2015-05-13 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180719173804/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/korbin-sims-resigns-with-knights-20130603-2nlso.html |archive-date=2018-07-19 |url-access=limited}} Sims made 12 appearances for the season. He was selected in the [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] 24-man squad along his brothers Ashton and Tariq for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]],{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/rugby-league-world-cup-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/75063/default.aspx |title=Rugby League World Cup squads |date=2013-10-14 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180719174049/https://www.nrl.com/news/2013/10/14/rugby-league-world-cup-squads/ |archive-date=2018-07-19 |access-date=2015-05-13}} playing in 3 matches and scoring a try in his Fiji international debut against [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the 32–14 win at [[Spotland Stadium]].{{Cite news |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/24694176 |title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: Fiji 32-14 Ireland |date=2013-10-28 |access-date=2015-05-13 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150423092202/https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/24694176 |archive-date=2015-04-23 |publisher=BBC}} ===2014=== In Round 7 against the [[Brisbane Broncos]], Sims scored his first NRL career try in Newcastle's 32–6 loss at [[Hunter Stadium]].{{Cite news |last=Toohey |first=Barry |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/brisbane-broncos-rocksolid-as-newcastle-knights-crumble-at-hunter-stadium/story-fni3fbgz-1226889502591 |title=Brisbane Broncos rock-solid as Newcastle Knights crumble at Hunter Stadium |date=2014-04-18 |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney |access-date=2015-05-13 }} In May, Sims played for Fiji in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations#Samoa v. Fiji|2014 Pacific Test]]. Fiji lost the test match 32–16, but Korbin stole the limelight with a massive shot on Samoan opponent [[Isaac Liu]].{{Cite news |last=Phelps |first=James |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/gutsy-samoa-secures-place-in-four-nations-tournament-following-win-over-fiji/story-fni3fbgz-1226904268873 |title=Gutsy Samoa secures place in Four Nations tournament following win over Fiji |date=2014-05-03 |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney |access-date=2015-05-13 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140506135849/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/gutsy-samoa-secures-place-in-four-nations-tournament-following-win-over-fiji/story-fni3fbgz-1226904268873 |archive-date=2014-05-06 }} Sims finished the [[2014 NRL season|year]] with him playing in 20 matches and scoring 2 tries. ===2015=== In the pre-season, Sims played for Newcastle in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]].{{Cite web |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/ |title=KNIGHTS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD |date=2015-01-20 |website=Rugby League Week |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150523054609/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/ |archive-date=2015-05-23 |access-date=2015-05-13}} On 13 March, he again re-signed with the Newcastle outfit on a two-year contract.{{Cite news |url=http://www.smh.com.au//breaking-news-sport/korbin-sims-resigns-with-knights-20150313-3s2j3.html |title=Korbin Sims re-signs with Knights |date=2015-03-13 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |access-date=2015-05-13 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130234832/https://www.smh.com.au/breaking-news-sport/korbin-sims-resigns-with-knights-20150313-3s2j3.html |archive-date=2016-01-30 |agency=Australian Associated Press}} On 2 May, he played for [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in the [[International rugby league in 2015#Melanesian Cup|2015 Melanesian Cup]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx |title=Representative Round team lists |date=2015-05-03 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150505202335/http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx |archive-date=2015-05-05 |access-date=2015-05-13}}{{Cite news |last=Garry |first=Chris |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/korbin-sims-storms-into-maroons-origin-contention-with-standout-performance-in-fijis-win-over-papua-new-guinea/story-fni3fbgz-1227332029470 |title=Korbin Sims Storms into Maroons Origin Contention with Stand Out Performance in Fiji's Win Over Papua New Guinea |date=2015-05-02 |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney |access-date=2015-12-20 }} On 10 May, Sims groped the genitals of opposition player and former teammate [[Willie Mason]], an action described by Rugby League writer Brad Walter as being "infantile", "bizarre and offensive", and worthy of punishment due to the potential for the incident to portray the NRL in a distasteful and damaging light.{{Cite news |last=Walter |first=Brad |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/newcastle-knights/korbin-sims-selection-for-maroons-as-damaging-for-nrl-as-willie-mason-incident-20150511-ggypf3.html |title=Korbin Sims selection as damaging for NRL as Willie Mason incident. |date=2015-05-11 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150512192116/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/newcastle-knights/korbin-sims-selection-for-maroons-as-damaging-for-nrl-as-willie-mason-incident-20150511-ggypf3.html |archive-date=2015-05-12 |url-access=limited}} Despite some rugby league reporters calling for harsh disciplinary action as a result of the incident, Mason was unperturbed, stating that he found the incident "hilarious."{{Cite news |last=Proszenko |first=Adrian |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/newcastle-knights-forward-korbin-sims-breaks-silence-on-williegate-20150513-gh0fii.html |title=Newcastle Knights forward Korbin Sims breaks silence on 'Williegate' |date=2015-05-13 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |access-date=2015-05-13 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150514030053/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/newcastle-knights-forward-korbin-sims-breaks-silence-on-williegate-20150513-gh0fii.html |archive-date=2015-05-14 }} Sims received a warning from the NRL match review committee. Sims apologized for any offence caused to those who were watching the game and described it as a "harmless gesture between two friends." On 10 June, Sims was named as 20th-man for the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] team for game 2 of the [[2015 State of Origin series]]. In the same match, his brother, Tariq, was named as 19th-man for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]].{{Cite news |last=Proszenko |first=Adrian |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/state-of-origin/father-defends-sims-boys-rights-to-take-divergent-origin-paths-20150613-ghn8yj.html |title=Father defends Sims boys' rights to take divergent Origin paths |date=2015-06-13 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130234832/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/state-of-origin/father-defends-sims-boys-rights-to-take-divergent-origin-paths-20150613-ghn8yj.html |archive-date=2016-01-30 }} However, neither of them played in the match. Sims finished off the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in 22 matches and scoring two tries.{{Cite web |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMjAyNDEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t |title=Custom Match List |date=2015-12-02 |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=2015-12-25}} ===2016=== On 12 January, Sims was selected in the [[Queensland Academy of Sport|QAS]] Emerging Maroons squad.{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/maroons-name-emerging-origin-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/91313/default.aspx |title=Maroons name Emerging Origin squad |last=Webeck |first=Tony |date=2016-01-12 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160114110023/http://www.nrl.com/maroons-name-emerging-origin-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/91313/default.aspx |archive-date=2016-01-14 |access-date=2016-01-15}} In February, he played for the Knights in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].{{Cite web |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/ |title=Knights Announce Nines Squad |date=2016-01-28 |website=Rugby League Week |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204042513/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/ |archive-date=2016-02-04 |access-date=2016-02-04}} He finished the [[2016 NRL season|2016 season]] having played in 22 matches and scoring three tries.{{Cite web |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY3Ny00LS0tLS0tMjAyNDEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t |title=Custom Match List |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=2016-09-10}} In October, he played for Fiji against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], starting at lock in the 20–18 win at [[Apia Park]].{{Cite news |last=Glover |first=Ben |url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-rugby-league-test-match-samoa-v-fiji/news-story/a89f6b0a380cb77665ca5b68f6a0d64a |title=Re-live the action: Tyrone Phillips intercept try snatches thriller for Fiji over Samoa |date=2016-10-08 |publisher=News.com.au |access-date=2017-03-05 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170305113830/http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-rugby-league-test-match-samoa-v-fiji/news-story/a89f6b0a380cb77665ca5b68f6a0d64a |archive-date=2017-03-05 }} ===2017=== In January, Sims signed a two-year contract with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] starting effective immediately, after being released from the final year of his Knights contract.{{Cite web |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/korbin-sims-joins-broncos-25012/ |title=Korbin Sims joins the Broncos |date=2017-01-23 |website=Zero Tackle |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170202013053/https://www.zerotackle.com/korbin-sims-joins-broncos-25012/ |archive-date=2017-02-02 |access-date=2017-01-23}}{{Cite news |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/knights/knights-prop-korbin-sims-will-join-the-broncos-effective-immediately/news-story/d329439ed140e468aee6ef8150eae637 |title=Knights prop Korbin Sims will join the Broncos effective immediately |date=2017-01-20 |work=The Courier-Mail |access-date=2017-01-20 |url-access=subscription}} He made his Brisbane debut in round 1 of the [[2017 NRL season|2017 season]], starting at prop against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], in Brisbane's 26–18 win at [[Endeavour Field|Shark Park]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-round-1-live-cronulla-sharks-vs-brisbane-broncos-highlights-results-photos/news-story/ab334feb9bc0cb608653c359c026e4d8 |title=Broncos down Sharks 26-18 |work=The Australian |access-date=2017-03-05 |url-access=subscription}} In round 6 against the [[Sydney Roosters]], he scored his first try for the Brisbane club in their 32–8 win at [[Lang Park|Suncorp Stadium]].{{Cite news |last=Badel |first=Peter |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/broncos-defeat-roosters-328-with-gusty-display-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/bf54e03a1c78d5fa8cfd719ba95de165 |title=Broncos defeat Roosters 32-8 with gutsy display at Suncorp Stadium |date=2017-04-06 |work=The Courier Mail |access-date=2017-06-21 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170719115601/http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/broncos-defeat-roosters-328-with-gusty-display-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/bf54e03a1c78d5fa8cfd719ba95de165 |archive-date=2017-07-19 |url-access=subscription}} On 6 May 2017, Sims played for Fiji against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the [[2017 Pacific Rugby League Tests|2017 Pacific Cup]], starting at lock in Fiji's 26–24 loss at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].{{Cite news |last=Ziaziaris |first=Simone |url=http://wwos.nine.com.au/2017/05/06/19/50/tonga-break-fiji-s-hearts-in-test-thriller |title=Late Latu try grabs Test win for Tonga |date=2017-05-06 |work=Wide World of Sports |access-date=2017-06-21 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170614142309/http://wwos.nine.com.au/2017/05/06/19/50/tonga-break-fiji-s-hearts-in-test-thriller |archive-date=2017-06-14 |publisher=Nine Digital |agency=Australian Associated Press}} In Round 25 against the [[Parramatta Eels]], Sims suffered a season ending arm injury in Brisbane's 52–34 loss at [[Suncorp Stadium]], later ruining his chances to play for Fiji in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/brisbane-prop-korbin-sims-ruled-out-for-the-rest-of-the-season-due-to-shoulder-injury/news-story/8e1413f2ef175fdaafce5fa48ae215ae |title=Season over for Korbin Sims |work=The Courier-Mail |url-access=subscription}}{{Cite news |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/korbin-sims-says-he-has-to-improve-on-his-2017-form-to-help-brisbane-to-a-title/news-story/8c598be1564188b59547a4c449bfa63d |title=Sims: I know I need to lift |work=The Courier-Mail |url-access=subscription}} Sims finished the [[2017 NRL season]] with him playing in 22 matches and scoring 5 tries for the Brisbane club. ===2018=== In Round 2, in Brisbane's Queensland derby match against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], Sims played his 100th NRL career match in the 24–20 win at [[Suncorp Stadium]].{{Cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/03/14/personal-jab-driving-brisbane-broncos-prop-korbin-sims-in-100th-nrl-game/ |title='Personal jab' driving Brisbane Broncos prop Korbin Sims in 100th NRL game |last=Gould |first=Joel |date=2018-03-14 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180718032018/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/03/14/personal-jab-driving-brisbane-broncos-prop-korbin-sims-in-100th-nrl-game/ |archive-date=2018-07-18 |access-date=2018-07-19}}{{Cite news |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/anthony-milford-kodi-nikorima-fire-as-broncos-edge-cowboys-in-local-derby/news-story/30deeb44474fd1cf997c942aa4112df3 |title=Passionate Bennett basks in Broncos glory |work=The Courier-Mail |url-access=subscription}} On 18 July, Sims signed a three-year deal to join the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] starting in 2019.{{Cite web |url=https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2018/07/19/sims-to-reunite-at-red-v/ |title=Sims's to reunite at Red V |last1=Walter |first1=Brad |last2=Walsh |first2=Dan |date=2018-07-18 |website=St George Illawarra Dragons |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180719113725/https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2018/07/19/sims-to-reunite-at-red-v/ |archive-date=2018-07-19 |access-date=2018-07-19}} On 9 September, Sims endured a horror afternoon on the field against his future side [[St. George Illawarra]] as his brother Tariq ran past him on three occasions to score a [[hat-trick]] in the club's 48–18 defeat in week one of the finals eliminating Brisbane from the competition.{{Cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/sep/09/blistering-first-half-dragons-performance-puts-broncos-out-of-nrl-finals-running |title=Blistering first-half Dragons performance puts Broncos out of NRL finals running |date=2018-09-09 |work=The Guardian |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180909115417/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/sep/09/blistering-first-half-dragons-performance-puts-broncos-out-of-nrl-finals-running |archive-date=2018-09-09 |agency=Australian Associated Press}} ===2019=== Sims made his debut for [[St. George Illawarra]] against his former club the [[Brisbane Broncos]] scoring a try in a 25–24 victory at [[Suncorp Stadium]]. The following week, Sims was taken from the field with what was later to be revealed as a broken arm. Sims had remained on the field for a further 15 minutes after breaking his arm despite being in obvious discomfort. Sims was later ruled out for 8 weeks.{{Cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/04/07/korbin-sims-plays-on-despite-suspected-broken-arm/ |title=Korbin Sims confirms broken arm |date=2019-04-08 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190408205655/https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/04/07/korbin-sims-plays-on-despite-suspected-broken-arm/ |archive-date=2019-04-08}} Sims made a total of 16 appearances for [[St. George Illawarra]] in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club suffered one of their worst ever seasons finishing in 15th place on the table.{{Cite web |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/paul-mcgregor-facing-the-axe-as-st-george-illawarra-dragons-coach-after-horror-season/1m4wnmg28k3j1mhmkd7qlvj2x/ |title=Paul McGregor facing the axe as St. George Illawarra Dragons coach following horror season |last=Chisholm |first=Ed |date=2019-09-02 |website=Sporting News |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190905113456/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/paul-mcgregor-facing-the-axe-as-st-george-illawarra-dragons-coach-after-horror-season/1m4wnmg28k3j1mhmkd7qlvj2x/ |archive-date=2019-09-05}} ===2020=== On 15 February, Sims suffered a broken arm whilst playing for [[St. George Illawarra]] in the pre-season [[NRL Nines]] tournament against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. Sims was seen by cameras leaving the field in emotional distress.{{Cite web |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/perth-nines/casualty-ward-momirovski-fighting-fit-for-storm-move-lowe-and-milford-pick-up-knocks/news-story/7f0e6c29579bfee283f3053f84edb9e0/ |title=Josh McGuire to miss Cowboys' season opener after scans confirm knee injury |date=2020-02-17 |work=Fox Sports |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200218054813/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/perth-nines/casualty-ward-momirovski-fighting-fit-for-storm-move-lowe-and-milford-pick-up-knocks/news-story/7f0e6c29579bfee283f3053f84edb9e0/ |archive-date=2020-02-18}} Sims was limited to only five games throughout the season as [[St. George Illawarra]] finished 13th on the table and missed out on the finals.{{Cite news |last=O'Loughlin |first=Liam |date=2020-10-26 |title=NRL 2020 Season Review: How will your side fare next year? |work=Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201026175328/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |archive-date=2020-10-26}} In November 2020, Sims signed a two-year deal with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].{{Cite news |last=Dinjaski |first=Melanie |date=2020-11-20 |title=Sims released by Dragons to make UK move |work=Wide World of Sports |publisher=Nine Digital |url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/korbin-sims-released-by-st-george-illawarra-dragons/aca55f4f-d42c-4859-91d0-e3fcc8682685 |url-status=live |access-date=2020-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201119210937/https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/korbin-sims-released-by-st-george-illawarra-dragons/aca55f4f-d42c-4859-91d0-e3fcc8682685 |archive-date=2020-11-19}}{{Cite news |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=2020-11-19 |title=Korbin Sims' first words after joining Hull KR from St George |language=en |work=Hull Daily Mail |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-sign-korbin-sims-4718805 |url-status=live |access-date=2020-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201119211646/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-sign-korbin-sims-4718805 |archive-date=2020-11-19}} ===2021=== Sims played 14 games for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the 2021 Super League season as the club came within one game of the grand final. ===2022=== On 29 August, it was announced that Sims would be one of eleven players who were to depart [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at the end of the 2022 season.{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/hull-kr-confirm-11-departures-including-former-nrl-trio-and-star-winger-58198/|title=Hull KR confirm 11 departures including former NRL trio and star winger|website=seriousaboutrl.com}} On 30 August, Sims was suspended for three matches by the RFL. On the same day, Sims announced his retirement from Rugby League.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/korbin-sims-hull-kr-retires/|title=Korbin Sims: Hull KR prop to retire at end of season|website=loverugbyleague.com}} Sims later came out of retirement to play for Fiji at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]]. Sims played for Fiji in their controversial quarter final loss to New Zealand at the [[MKM Stadium]]. After the match, Sims announced his retirement for a second time.{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/korbin-sims-hull-kr-fiji-7789549/|title=Korbin Sims proud to have played final game in Hull after Fiji's exit from World Cup|website=hulldailymail.co.uk}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links==
